summary:
Blue Guardian scored 45/100 in our full review. The firm offers a broad range of challenge types — Instant Starter, Instant Standard, One Step, Two Step Standard, Two Step Pro, Three Step, Guardian X, and a dedicated Crypto line — with no time limits on evaluations and flexible platform access via MT5, Match-Trader, and TradeLocker.

Profit splits depend on the model: Instant Funding Standard is 80% by default (90% with add-on), Instant Starter is 90%, and evaluation-based funded accounts are up to 90%, though some large funded accounts are 85%. Payout timing also varies — instant accounts use instant payouts, 1 Step and some funded models can pay up to weekly, 2 Step Standard is bi-weekly, and 2 Step Pro offers first payout on demand then bi-weekly after.

Drawdown and daily loss rules are model-specific rather than a flat firm-wide rule. News trading is allowed during evaluations but restricted on funded accounts (5-minute window around high-impact events), with Guardian X as the exception where news trading is allowed at all stages. The firm highlights 83,000+ traders, 160,000+ community members across 160+ countries, and $20M+ in total rewards. However, the TrustPilot profile remains flagged for guideline violations, and all funded accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds in a simulated environment. Traders should review each model's specific terms before committing.
pros: Eight challenge types including Instant, One Step, Two Step, Three Step, Guardian X, and dedicated Crypto line
No time limits on any evaluation
MT5, Match-Trader, and TradeLocker platform options
Scaling plan to $4,000,000 — 12% profit in 3-month period triggers 25% account size increase
Active community with 160,000+ members across 160+ countries
Separate Crypto challenge line with accounts up to $100K and 1:4 leverage
24/7 support promoted with Guardian TV live streams and YouTube content
cons: TrustPilot profile flagged for guideline violations — rating unavailable
All funded accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds in a simulated environment
Rules vary significantly between challenge types — drawdown, split, payout schedule, and consistency all model-specific
No published data on spreads or execution quality
Guardian Shield auto-closes trades at 1–2% floating loss — repeated breaches can reduce split or close account
Instant Starter limited to one payout only, capped at 5% of initial balance
bestFor: Traders who want flexible evaluation options with no time limits, dedicated crypto and Guardian X challenge lines, and are comfortable navigating model-specific rules across instant and evaluation-based models
notIdealFor: Traders seeking consistent rules across all account types, published spread data, strong trust signals, or those uncomfortable with a firm whose TrustPilot rating has been removed and all funded accounts are demo-based

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Blue Guardian
verdictBlocks.body:
Blue Guardian scored 45/100 overall. The firm offers genuine variety across its challenge types — Instant Starter, Instant Standard, One Step, Two Step Standard, Two Step Pro, Three Step, Guardian X, and a dedicated Crypto line — and the no-time-limit evaluations give traders flexibility to pass without deadline pressure.

Profit splits depend on the model: Instant Funding Standard is 80% by default (90% with add-on), Instant Starter is 90%, Two Step Standard funded accounts are 90%, and Two Step Pro is also 90%. Payout schedules vary too — instant accounts pay instantly, 1 Step and Three Step can pay weekly, 2 Step Standard is bi-weekly, and 2 Step Pro offers first payout on demand then bi-weekly. The scaling plan is published: traders can scale to $4,000,000 by making 12% in a 3-month period, after which account size increases by 25% of the initial balance. Up to $400K in funded accounts is allowed.

The main structural concern is that rules vary significantly by model. Daily drawdown, max drawdown, consistency rules, minimum trading days, payout caps, and news trading restrictions all depend on which model you're on. Guardian Shield auto-closes trades when floating loss hits 1–2% of initial balance on many funded accounts, and repeated breaches can reduce your split or close the account. The 2-minute minimum holding time, 30-day inactivity rule, and gambling rule (margin above 80%) apply across models.

The firm highlights 83,000+ traders, 160,000+ community members, 160+ countries, and $20M+ in total rewards. But the TrustPilot profile remains flagged, and all funded accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds in a simulated environment. For traders who can navigate model-specific rules, Blue Guardian offers competitive pricing and genuine flexibility. But traders should closely review the specific terms of their chosen model before committing.

faq.a: Yes, Blue Guardian does pay out, but payout terms vary by model. Instant accounts use instant payouts. 1 Step and some funded models can pay up to weekly. 2 Step Standard is bi-weekly. 2 Step Pro offers first payout on demand, then bi-weekly after. Profit splits range from 80% to 90% depending on the account type — Instant Standard is 80% by default (90% with add-on). Payouts are processed through Rise via bank transfer or crypto, with crypto withdrawals capped at $2,000 per request. Minimum withdrawal is $100. All trades must be closed before requesting a payout.

faq.q: What is the most legit prop firm?
faq.a: The most legit prop firm in our rankings is DNA Funded, which scored 97/100 based on live testing. It's ranked number one on our best prop firms list for offering low-cost challenges, strong broker backing, and up to 90% profit splits. By comparison, Blue Guardian scored 45/100 in our review, largely due to model-specific rule complexity, a flagged TrustPilot profile, and the fact that all funded accounts are demo-based. If legitimacy and structure matter, start with a firm that's tested and verified.

faq.q: Which broker does Blue Guardian use?
faq.a: Blue Guardian is not a broker-backed prop firm. All funded accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds in a simulated environment. This doesn't automatically make it untrustworthy, but it does mean less transparency compared to broker-backed prop firms. Broker-backed firms often offer better execution quality and clearer trading conditions, which can be a key factor for serious traders evaluating cost and slippage.

Guardian Shield is a funded-stage risk management feature. On many funded accounts it auto-closes open trades when floating PnL reaches 1% or 2% loss of initial balance. Repeated breaches of Guardian Shield can lead to a reduced profit split or account closure. This is one of the most discussed rules among Blue Guardian traders and can impact strategies that require holding through drawdowns.

flaggedWarning: Blue Guardian's TrustPilot profile has been **flagged for guideline violations**, making their public rating unavailable. All funded accounts are **demo accounts with virtual funds in a simulated environment**. Key rules — including payout caps, consistency requirements, drawdown types, minimum trading days, and news trading restrictions — **vary significantly between challenge models**. Guardian Shield auto-closes trades at 1–2% floating loss on many funded accounts, and repeated breaches can reduce your split or close the account. Traders should review each model's specific terms before committing.
